meaning: v.反叛,造反 vbl.反叛,造反
examples: Voters rebelled against high property taxes.
She rebelled against the regimentation of school life.
Forty members of the governing Conservative party rebelled, voting against the government line.
Provoked by the rash of corporate scandals, shareholders in company after company rebelled against management this past spring.
Florida in 1987 and Massachusetts in 1991 repealed laws taxing services after taxpayers rebelled.
